description:
Goal
Compiler2 should do runtime work only for values that are demanded by an executable boundary. Exact callable use is semantic evidence, not representation obligation. The compiler must preserve the exact callable surfaces it already discovers and derive all internal transport, ABI lanes, callable entries, native callable boundaries, and closure materialization only from settled runtime demand.

Why this replaces the narrow bug
The old symptom was ABI-ready programs publishing callable_entries for closure bodies even when no call or escape demanded them. That is not a local ABI bug. It is a modeling bug: compiler2 discovers exact callable use, throws it away, then later recovers runtime obligations from types or from narrower transport models. That recovery is inherently too broad and will keep producing unused callable constructors, extra callable entries, wrong closure surfaces, ABI leaks, and now, as we have learned, return/resume transport bugs.

Core distinction
Semantic fact: which callable surface is actually used? The source of truth is CallSiteSummary.targets[*].surface_inputs plus exact escape/opaque evidence.
Representation fact: which callable values or structural lanes must physically exist at runtime? This is derived later from runtime demand.

Correct model
Add a root/executable-scoped runtime demand plan. ActivationKey.input remains semantic. Semantic activation inputs do not imply runtime ABI lanes. Runtime transport layout is derived from demand after semantic closure is sealed.

Demand vocabulary
RuntimeDemand = Ignore | Value | TupleFields(Vec<RuntimeDemand>) | Callable(CallableDemand).
CallableDemand preserves exact demand as resolved surfaces, opaque, and escape. Resolved(surface_inputs) means a direct callable invocation needs that callable surface. Opaque means the callable reaches a boundary that can call it with unknown shape. Escape means a first-class callable value must survive as data.

Transport rule
There must be one shared internal transport model for runtime-carried values. Inputs, direct call args, continuation captures, executable returns, and delivered-resume inputs must all use the same demand-derived layout family. The layout family must be able to represent at least:
- omitted
- one runtime value lane
- tuple-field lanes
- direct-callable captures with exact target identity

The exact Rust names can differ, but the invariant is mandatory: internal return/resume transport may not collapse back to a narrower model than input transport.

Representation derivation
Only after runtime demand settles, derive:
- internal boundary transport layouts
- ABI-ready callable entries
- native callable boundaries
- backend/native/codegen closure materialization

Direct-only resolved callable demand may pass executable identity plus demanded captures without allocating or publishing a first-class closure. Opaque or escaping callable demand requires first-class callable materialization and a callable-entry boundary.

What we now know
The first half of the plan is working: inputs and captures can be transported from demand without heap closure materialization. The remaining red proved the missing seam. Return/resume transport still uses the old narrower ReturnAbi model, so a direct-only callable can survive semantic analysis, runtime-demand closure, and input transport, then collapse back into ValueRef at DeliveredResume. That is not a separate bug category. It is the same modeling bug at the return boundary.

Removal target
Remove callable-specific and boundary-specific recovery paths:
- runtime_callable_values, runtime_callable_boundary_values, note_callable_arg_uses, ClosureBoundaryUse::CallableArg, closure_value_needs_runtime_boundary, resolve_materialized_runtime_callable_boundary_activations
- ABI-ready callable entry derivation from arbitrary callable value types
- native callable boundary selection from capture types
- native callable identity collection from all callable-producing steps
- backend/native special-case resume recomputation once shared boundary transport exists
- the assumption that executable returns can be modeled by a smaller transport vocabulary than inputs

Signal
Existing quicksort tuple-field delivery must remain green. compiler2_abi_ready_does_not_publish_unused_callable_constructors must pass for the right reason: no demand, no callable entry. Enum.reduce and related closure-call fixtures must pass because direct-only callables stay direct across call, return, and resume boundaries. Telemetry should expose demand counts, omitted lanes, direct-callable transport, and first-class materialization separately.

Strategy
Work data-model-up. First model demand, then compute the demand closure, then derive shared boundary transport from that one plan, then derive callable materialization and external publication from the same facts, then remove backend/native/interpreter recovery logic that was compensating for missing transport facts. Each child ticket is one green commit and closes after its checks pass.

Design reference
See proposal.html for the worked examples. Tickets below carry the required data structures, algorithms, removals, and checks so the plan survives without local context.
title: Compiler2 runtime value demand: derive ABI and callable work from demand
